profile.name: Sai Karthik K
profile.tagline: Building intelligent systems at the hardware–software boundary
profile.specialization: Embedded Systems · IoT · Firmware · Edge AI
profile.bio: M.Tech student in Embedded Systems Technology at SRMIST, with hands-on experience across firmware development, RTOS, embedded Linux, sensor interfacing, computer vision, and edge AI. I like taking a system from a bare schematic to a working, intelligent product — currently building a federated-learning intrusion detection system that runs on an ESP32.

experience.summary: Supported board-level design and validation for production-bound embedded hardware.
highlights: Assisted in PCB design, schematic analysis, component placement, and layout verification.
Supported prototype assembly, testing, and hardware validation for embedded boards.
Performed circuit debugging and troubleshooting to ensure reliable hardware performance.

experience.summary: Built firmware and integrated sensors for real-time embedded applications.
highlights: Assisted in developing Embedded C firmware and hardware–software integrated applications.
Integrated sensors and peripherals for real-time embedded system development.
Performed debugging, testing, and validation to ensure reliable system performance.

projects.title: FL-IDS: Federated Learning Intrusion Detection
projects.subtitle: M.Tech Capstone · in progress
projects.description: A federated-learning intrusion detection system with quantization-aware training, designed to run directly on ESP32 microcontrollers. Built on a research gap identified across CICIoT2023, N-BaIoT, and related IoT-attack datasets.

projects.title: Energy-Aware Adaptive Irrigation Control
projects.subtitle: Embedded Systems & IoT
projects.description: An ESP32-based smart irrigation system with adaptive control driven by real-time soil moisture data and energy-efficient scheduling via deep sleep mode.

projects.title: Smart Indoor Environment Monitoring System
projects.subtitle: Embedded Systems & IoT
projects.description: A multi-sensor ESP32 system tracking indoor air quality with a computed Indoor Health Index (IHI) and automated alerts when conditions drift.

projects.title: AI Voice Assistant Using ESP32
projects.subtitle: IoT & AI
projects.description: An ESP32-based voice assistant for real-time command recognition and device control, integrating voice recognition with wireless communication for smart-home automation.

projects.title: Hand Gesture-Based Volume Control
projects.subtitle: Computer Vision & AI
projects.description: A real-time, touch-free volume control system using hand tracking and gesture recognition.
